variants.description: Create a multi-step wizard using CardLayout!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: cardPanel.add(panel, "name"); then cardLayout.show(cardPanel, "name") to switch between cards!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: BoxLayout for Vertical/Horizontal Arrangement
variants.description: Use BoxLayout to create flexible vertical and horizontal arrangements!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: new BoxLayout(panel, BoxLayout.Y_AXIS) for vertical. Use Box.createVerticalStrut(pixels) for spacing!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: SpringLayout for Constraint-Based Positioning
variants.description: Use SpringLayout to position components with flexible constraints!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: layout.putConstraint(SpringLayout.WEST, textField, 10, SpringLayout.EAST, label); positions textField relative to label!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Custom Layout Manager
variants.description: Create a simple custom layout manager!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: In layoutContainer, loop through components and position them in a circle using Math.cos/sin!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Responsive Layout with ComponentListener
variants.description: Create a layout that adapts to window resize!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: frame.addComponentListener(new ComponentAdapter() { public void componentResized(ComponentEvent e) { if (frame.getWidth() < 600) { ... } } });

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Split Pane for Resizable Sections
variants.description: Use JSplitPane to create resizable panels!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: JSplitPane splitPane = new JSplitPane(JSplitPane.HORIZONTAL_SPLIT, leftComp, rightComp); splitPane.setDividerLocation(200)!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: GroupLayout for Form Design
variants.description: Use GroupLayout to create professional forms with alignment!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: GroupLayout aligns components automatically. Create parallel groups for columns and sequential groups for rows!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Overlay Layout with JLayeredPane
variants.description: Create overlapping components with different layers!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: layeredPane.add(component, JLayeredPane.DEFAULT_LAYER); Higher layer numbers appear on top!

variants.description: Combine BorderLayout and GridLayout for a login form!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Use BorderLayout for frame. Create separate panels for form fields (GridLayout) and buttons (FlowLayout). Combine them!

variants.description: Create a multi-page application using CardLayout!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: cardPanel.add(page1, "page1"); to add pages. Use cardLayout.show(cardPanel, "page1"); to navigate!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: JDBC Connection with PreparedStatement
variants.description: Use PreparedStatement to safely query a database and prevent SQL injection!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement("SELECT * FROM table WHERE col = ?"); stmt.setString(1, value); ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery();

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: ResultSet Navigation and Metadata
variants.description: Navigate ResultSet and get metadata about columns!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: ResultSetMetaData metaData = rs.getMetaData(); int columns = metaData.getColumnCount(); while (rs.next()) { ... }

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Batch Inserts for Better Performance
variants.description: Use batch processing to insert multiple records efficiently!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: conn.setAutoCommit(false); Loop: stmt.setString(); stmt.addBatch(); After loop: stmt.executeBatch(); conn.commit();

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Stored Procedure Call
variants.description: Call a stored procedure using CallableStatement!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: CallableStatement stmt = conn.prepareCall("{CALL proc_name(?, ?)}"); stmt.registerOutParameter(2, Types.INTEGER); stmt.execute(); int result = stmt.getInt(2);

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Transaction Management
variants.description: Manage database transactions with commit and rollback!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: conn.setAutoCommit(false); Execute statements; If success: conn.commit(); If error: conn.rollback();

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Connection Pooling Basics
variants.description: Implement a simple connection pool for better performance!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Create connections in constructor. getConnection() moves from available to used. releaseConnection() moves back!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: ResultSet Types and Concurrency
variants.description: Use scrollable and updatable ResultSets!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: createStatement(ResultSet.TYPE_SCROLL_INSENSITIVE, ResultSet.CONCUR_UPDATABLE); Then use rs.last(), rs.previous(), rs.updateString(), rs.updateRow()!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: BLOB Handling - Store and Retrieve Images
variants.description: Store and retrieve images (BLOB data) in database!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Store: stmt.setBinaryStream(1, new FileInputStream(path)); Retrieve: Blob blob = rs.getBlob("col"); Write blob.getBytes() to FileOutputStream!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Database Metadata Exploration
variants.description: Explore database structure using DatabaseMetaData!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: DatabaseMetaData metaData = conn.getMetaData(); ResultSet tables = metaData.getTables(null, null, "%", new String[]{"TABLE"}); Process ResultSet!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: RowSet for Disconnected Data Access
variants.description: Use CachedRowSet for disconnected data operations!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: CachedRowSet works disconnected. Set URL, username, password, command, execute(). Data is cached. Modify and acceptChanges() to sync!

variants.title: Level 3: Insert Single Record
variants.description: Insert a student record into the database!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: PreparedStatement pstmt = conn.prepareStatement(sql); pstmt.setString(1, value); pstmt.setInt(2, age); int rows = pstmt.executeUpdate();

variants.title: Level 3: Query All Records
variants.description: Retrieve and display all students from the database!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery("SELECT * FROM students"); while(rs.next()) { int id = rs.getInt("id"); String name = rs.getString("name"); }

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Dynamic CRUD with PreparedStatement
variants.description: Build a complete CRUD system with PreparedStatement for Students!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Create 4 methods: createStudent(), readAllStudents(), updateStudent(), deleteStudent(). Use PreparedStatement for all!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Search and Filter with WHERE Clause
variants.description: Implement advanced search functionality with multiple filters!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Use LIKE with % for pattern matching. Use BETWEEN for ranges. Combine conditions with AND/OR!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Sorting and Pagination
variants.description: Implement ORDER BY and LIMIT for sorted, paginated results!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: LIMIT controls page size, OFFSET skips records. offset = (pageNumber - 1) * pageSize. ORDER BY sorts results!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Aggregate Functions and Grouping
variants.description: Use COUNT, AVG, SUM, MAX, MIN with GROUP BY!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Aggregate functions: COUNT(*), AVG(column), SUM(column), MAX(column), MIN(column). Use GROUP BY to group results!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: JOIN Operations for Related Data
variants.description: Use INNER JOIN to combine data from multiple tables!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Use table aliases (s, e). INNER JOIN syntax: FROM table1 t1 INNER JOIN table2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.foreign_id!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Transaction-Safe Batch Updates
variants.description: Perform multiple updates in a single transaction with rollback!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: conn.setAutoCommit(false); Execute multiple updates; If all succeed: conn.commit(); If error: conn.rollback()!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Bulk Insert with CSV Import
variants.description: Import data from CSV file and insert into database!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Read CSV line by line, split by comma, parse values, add to batch. Execute batch periodically for performance!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Export to CSV with Formatted Data
variants.description: Export database records to CSV file with proper formatting!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Use PrintWriter to write CSV. Write header first, then loop through ResultSet and write each row with comma separators!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Subqueries for Complex Filtering
variants.description: Use subqueries to find data based on calculated values!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Subquery in parentheses: WHERE column > (SELECT AVG(column) FROM table). Inner query executes first!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Upsert - Insert or Update
variants.description: Implement INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE for upsert operations!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: INSERT ... VALUES ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE col1 = ?, col2 = ?. Set values for both INSERT and UPDATE!

variants.title: Level 3: Batch INSERT Operations
variants.description: Insert multiple records efficiently using batch processing!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Disable auto-commit, add multiple statements with addBatch(), execute all with executeBatch(), then commit()!

variants.title: Level 3: Conditional SELECT with Filters
variants.description: Query products with multiple filter conditions!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Build SQL dynamically based on choice. Use BETWEEN for ranges, LIKE with % for partial match, < for comparison!

variants.description: Implement transactions with rollback for order processing!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Transaction ensures all steps succeed or all fail. Check stock first, then insert order, update quantity, commit. Rollback if any step fails!

variants.hint: 💡 Hint: After each CRUD operation, insert a record in audit_log table with action type, timestamp, and details. Track all changes!

variants.description: Create custom exception classes for specific error scenarios!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Custom exception extends Exception. Add fields to store context. Throw with: throw new CustomException("message");

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Try-with-Resources for Auto-Closing
variants.description: Use try-with-resources to automatically close resources!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: try (Resource r1 = new Resource1(); Resource r2 = new Resource2()) { ... } Resources auto-close, no finally needed!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Exception Chaining and Root Cause
variants.description: Chain exceptions to preserve original error context!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Chain exceptions: throw new CustomException("message", originalException); Use getCause() to access root cause!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Multi-Catch for Similar Handling
variants.description: Handle multiple exception types with single catch block!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Multi-catch syntax: catch (ExceptionType1 | ExceptionType2 e) { ... } Use | to separate exception types!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 1: Rethrowing with Enhanced Context
variants.description: Catch, log, add context, and rethrow exceptions!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Catch exception, log with context, then rethrow: throw e; Caller can handle or log additionally!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Exception Hierarchy for Domain
variants.description: Create an exception hierarchy for your application domain!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Create base AppException, extend for specific domains (Validation, DataAccess, BusinessRule). Catch most specific first!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Comprehensive Logging Strategy
variants.description: Implement structured logging with different levels!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Logger levels: SEVERE (errors), WARNING, INFO, CONFIG, FINE (debug). Use logger.log(Level.SEVERE, msg, exception) for exceptions!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Graceful Degradation Pattern
variants.description: Implement fallback mechanisms when operations fail!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Try primary operation, if fails try backup, if both fail return cached/default value. Never let exception crash the app!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Circuit Breaker Pattern
variants.description: Implement circuit breaker to prevent cascading failures!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Circuit Breaker states: CLOSED (normal), OPEN (blocking calls), HALF_OPEN (testing recovery). Track failures and timeout!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 2: Assertion and Defensive Programming
variants.description: Use assertions and validation for defensive programming!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Use assert for internal checks (preconditions, postconditions, invariants). Throw exceptions for public API validation. Run with -ea to enable!

variants.title: Level 3: Prepared Statement Security (SQL Injection Prevention)
variants.description: Demonstrate why PreparedStatement prevents SQL injection!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Show concatenation first, then PreparedStatement. Explain that PreparedStatement escapes special characters automatically!

variants.title: Level 3: Connection Pooling Simulation
variants.description: Implement a simple connection pool for better performance!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Create connections upfront. getConnection() moves from available to used. releaseConnection() moves back. Reuse connections for better performance!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 3 Activity 3: Exception chaining
variants.description: Practice Exception chaining for level 3!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Focus on implementing Exception chaining step by step!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 3 Activity 4: Exception chaining
variants.description: Practice Exception chaining for level 3!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Focus on implementing Exception chaining step by step!

variants.title: Intermediate Level 3 Activity 5: Exception chaining
variants.description: Practice Exception chaining for level 3!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Focus on implementing Exception chaining step by step!

variants.title: Level 4: Comprehensive Error Logging System
variants.description: Create a robust error logging system for database operations!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Use PrintWriter with append mode. Format: [timestamp] [OPERATION] Error: message (State: XX000, Code: 1234). Test different SQL errors!

variants.title: Level 4: Transaction Rollback Best Practices
variants.description: Implement proper transaction handling with savepoints!
variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Savepoint sp = conn.setSavepoint("name"); If error, conn.rollback(sp); rolls back to that point only, not entire transaction!

variants.hint: 💡 Hint: Focus on implementing Logging strategies step by step!
